_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize SW Florida House batch HD-78..82 (Lee/Collier). All R incumbents. Currency: Persons-Mulicka VACATED HD-78 (resigned May 2026 -> Lee County Supervisor of Elections) -> former; Melo running UP to FL Senate District 28 -> running_higher_office. Scored from FL roll calls (flsenate/flhouse), Freedom Index, Digital Democracy, Equality FL / FL Right to Life ratings, and cited campaign statements.

set.office: State Representative, District 78 (resigned May 2026 — appointed Lee County Supervisor of Elections)

profile.background: Attorney (admitted 2012; business/real-estate/zoning law). BAs from Evangel University (Assemblies of God national university); JD George Washington U. Lead House sponsor of FL's 2023 Heartbeat Protection Act.

jenna-persons-mulicka@FL.notes_set: CURRENCY: Resigned from FL House on 2026-05-20 when Gov. DeSantis appointed her Lee County Supervisor of Elections; HD-78 vacated; NOT a 2026 House candidate. Scored on record. LIFE: lead House sponsor of the 2023 Heartbeat Protection Act (HB 7/SB 300, 6-week ban); public quotes 'life at conception' and 'a heartbeat is irrefutable evidence of life.' MARRIAGE/SCHOOLS: rated anti-equality by Equality Florida on her 2026 education package. ELECTIONS: sponsored HB 991 (2026) requiring proof of citizenship to register + tightened voter ID. No CBDC/2A/euthanasia/federalism evidence -> null. No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Lead House sponsor of 2023 6-week Heartbeat Protection Act; public 'life at conception' statement.

4.note: Equality Florida rates her anti-equality, citing her 2026 education package opposing their sexual-health-education provisions.

1.note: Sponsored HB 991 (2026): proof of U.S. citizenship to register + tightened photo voter ID (removed student IDs).

profile.background: FL Army National Guard Chief Warrant Officer (CW2), Iraq combat veteran; founder of cybersecurity / law-enforcement software firms. Freedom Index lifetime ~75%.

mike-giallombardo@FL.notes_set: Confirmed sitting HD-79 (Cape Coral/Lee) running for reelection 2026. LIFE: co-sponsored HB 7 (2023) heartbeat package + HB 351 (2021) pain-capable. MARRIAGE: co-sponsored HB 1475 (2021) biological-sex women's sports. FAMILY: co-sponsored HB 1 (2023) universal school choice. ECON: co-sponsored HB 3 (2023) anti-ESG. JUSTICE: primary sponsor HB 935 'Chiefs of Police'; career building law-enforcement software. FEDERALISM: sponsored FL State Guard bills (HB 1285/HB 1551). BORDER: voted YES SB 264 (2023) foreign-farmland ban; co-sponsored HB 1355 immigration enforcement. 2A: co-introduced HB 543 (2023) permitless carry; firearms preemption + lowered purchase age. NRA-endorsed 2022. No cross-party deviation; null where unevidenced (election method, CBDC, euthanasia, abolition).

0.note: Co-sponsored HB 7 (2023) heartbeat-package + HB 351 (2021) pain-capable unborn child protection.

2.note: Co-sponsored HB 1475 (2021) sex-specific student athletics (biological-sex women's sports).

4.note: Co-sponsored HB 3 (2023) 'Government and Corporate Activism' anti-ESG bill.

0.note: Primary sponsor HB 935 (2023) 'Chiefs of Police'; founded law-enforcement software firm — affirmative pro-police advocacy.

3.note: Sponsored FL State Guard bills (HB 1285 2023, HB 1551 2024) — state military force independent of federal control; affirms state sovereignty.

4.note: Voted YES SB 264 (2023) restricting foreign (China-aligned) ownership of FL farmland/real property.

0.note: Co-introduced HB 543 (2023), Florida's permitless/constitutional carry law.

1.note: Co-sponsored HB 1409 (2021) firearms preemption; voted to lower minimum purchase age (HB 759) — opposes gun restrictions/registries.

profile.background: Small-business owner (VP, family marina/boat-club business in Bonita Springs). Chairs Natural Resources & Disasters Subcommittee. Reputation as business-moderate with a water-quality focus; no documented roll-call deviation from the GOP base.

adam-botana@FL.notes_set: Confirmed sitting HD-80 (Lee/Collier) running for reelection 2026. LIFE: voted YES SB 300 (2023) 6-week heartbeat ban (roll-call PDF). MARRIAGE/FAMILY: voted YES CS/SB 254 (2023) banning gender-transition care for minors + parental-consent framework. BORDER: voted YES SB 1718 (2023) sanctuary preemption + mandatory E-Verify (roll-call PDF). 2A: co-introducer of HB 543 (2023) permitless carry; 'God-given right' floor speech; NRA-endorsed 2022. Religion Catholic (FL House page). Business-moderate reputation is on water/environment (outside this rubric); null where unevidenced.

0.note: Voted YES on SB 300 (2023) 6-week Heartbeat Protection Act (House roll call, 'Y Botana').

2.note: Voted YES CS/SB 254 (2023) felony ban on gender-transition care for minors.

1.note: SB 254 (2023) establishes parental-consent framework over minors' gender interventions.

3.note: Voted YES SB 1718 (2023): mandatory E-Verify for employers.

0.note: Co-introducer of HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry; 'constitutional God-given right' floor speech; NRA-endorsed 2022.

profile.background: U.S. Air Force veteran; licensed realtor / landscaping-business co-owner. Chaired Collier County Moms for Liberty 2022–24. First elected 2024; thinner roll-call record. (Religion Jewish per Ballotpedia/Wikipedia aggregators.)

yvette-benarroch@FL.notes_set: Confirmed sitting HD-81 (Collier/Naples–Marco) running for reelection 2026; first elected 2024 so record is thin. LIFE: co-sponsored HB 289 (2026) wrongful-death-of-an-unborn-child (defines unborn child as Homo sapiens 'at any stage'); endorsed by Florida Right to Life (2024); public praise for SB 300 6-week ban. SCHOOLS: chaired Collier County Moms for Liberty (parental-rights / anti-gender-ideology curriculum advocacy). Religion is Jewish (aggregator-sourced) — Christian-liberty cells left null, NOT inferred. Areas with no evidence (elections, justice, federalism, CBDC, 2A specifics, border specifics) left null; campaign 2A/border statements too vague to score.

0.note: Co-sponsored HB 289 (2026) defining unborn child as a living human at any developmental stage; endorsed by Florida Right to Life; public praise for the 6-week ban.

4.note: Chaired Collier County Moms for Liberty (2022–24) — affirmative campaign against LGBTQ/gender-ideology content in public schools.

2.note: Moms for Liberty chair opposing CRT/SOGI/gender-ideology curricula in K-12.

set.office: State Representative, District 82 (sitting · 2026 candidate for FL Senate District 28)

profile.background: Founder of a trucking company; real-estate broker (2020 president, Naples Area Board of Realtors). Sitting HD-82 rep running for FL Senate District 28 in 2026 (R primary vs. Rep. Bob Rommel). Digital Democracy rates her ~62% on conservative state positions — diverges on some fiscal/procedural votes (outside this rubric).

lauren-melo@FL.notes_set: CURRENCY: still sitting HD-82 (Collier) but running UP to FL Senate District 28 in 2026 (contested R primary). LIFE: YES on SB 300 (6-week ban), HB 7, HB 1517 (wrongful death of unborn). MARRIAGE/FAMILY: authored HB 743 (2026) targeting aiding/abetting of minor gender-transition care; YES on HB 1421/SB 1674; YES on universal school choice (HB 1/HB 3C) + charter schools. CHRISTIAN LIBERTY: YES on HB 1009 religious expression in K-12. BORDER: YES on SB 1718 + SB 2B immigration enforcement (E-Verify, anti-sanctuary). 2A: YES on pro-gun bills incl. HB 1615 (firearms during emergencies). Overall record moderate on fiscal/procedural votes (not in rubric) — no cross-party deviation on life/marriage/border; null where unevidenced.

0.note: Voted YES on SB 300 (6-week ban), HB 7 (abortion), and HB 1517 (wrongful death of an unborn child).

2.note: Authored HB 743 (2026) targeting minor gender-transition care; YES on HB 1421 gender clinical interventions + SB 1674 sex-based facilities.

0.note: Voted YES on universal school choice (HB 1 / HB 3C Family Empowerment Scholarship) and HB 443 charter schools.

1.note: Authored HB 743 (2026) expanding restrictions/parental protections against minor gender-transition interventions.

3.note: Voted YES on HB 1009 'Religious Expression in K-12 Schools.'

2.note: Voted YES on SB 1718 (2023) and SB 2B immigration enforcement — sanctuary preemption.

3.note: Voted YES on SB 1718 / SB 2B mandatory E-Verify provisions.

1.note: Voted YES on pro-gun bills incl. HB 1615 (firearms during states of emergency) — opposes citizen disarmament.
